name: Sicherheitsfahrschaltung (Sifa)
summary: Sifa checks that the driver remains responsive. It is separate from signalling and train protection.

playerModel.coreRule: Reset Sifa regularly and respond immediately to its warning. A missed response causes a brake intervention.

states.action: Continue driving and operate the Sifa control as required by the vehicle.

states.recognition: A Sifa warning lamp or message appears.
An audible warning sounds after the visual warning was not answered.
The train applies a penalty or emergency brake.
states.action: Allow braking to complete as required, reset Sifa, and follow the locomotive-specific recovery procedure.

importantDistinctions: Sifa does not tell you whether a signal may be passed.
Sifa does not replace PZB, LZB or ETCS.
The pedal, button, release timing and reset behaviour differ between trains.

tswNotes: Control bindings and cab controls vary between locomotives and multiple-units.
Some vehicles use an intermittent press-and-release rhythm while others are represented differently in TSW.
The universal guide therefore explains the warning sequence but leaves exact control timing to train-specific pages.
